one_time_description: Loss on transfer of tangible capital assets (disposal of Phase 5 assets)
economic_notes: Bonaparte Sales Limited Partnership is a government business enterprise with contributions of $3,268,769 during the year. Nation also holds investment in 1049280 BC Ltd. Natural Resources and Economic Development is an active segment.
notes: Audit is qualified on three grounds: (1) PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations not assessed; (2) Bonaparte Sales Limited Partnership GBE reported under ASPE rather than IFRS as required; (3) assessment not completed as to whether Bonaparte Sales LP meets PS 1300 criteria for modified equity method. No schedule of remuneration provided. Province of BC Daycare deferred revenue of $4,942,792 is a large new item in 2024. Total government transfers estimated as ISC $9,130,645 + ISC recoveries $71,845 + FNHA $889,546 + Government of Canada $169,998 + CMHC $23,815 + CMHC $75,000 + Province of BC $4,099,701 = $14,460,550. OSR calculated as total revenue $18,590,524 minus government transfers $14,460,550 = $4,129,974. Loss on transfer of capital assets $766,020 relates to disposal of Phase 5 assets. Consolidated entity includes Bonaparte First Nation Social Housing. GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: Bonaparte Sales Limited Partnership and 1049280 BC Ltd.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
